Details: E. coli O157:H7 inactivation with chlorine is dependent on temperature, pH, chlorine dose, and contact time. However, all experiments indicated that chlorine contact times commonly used in drinking water treatment inactivate E. coli O157:H7 to safe levels. For example, 1 to 4 log destruction was observed when chlorine residual levels ranged from 0.25 to 2 mg/L and contact times ranged from 0.5 to 5 minutes.
